findings.description: charge_order sets status='paid' but never writes paid_at, yet monthly_revenue_report filters on strftime(paid_at); every order paid via this path has paid_at=NULL and is silently excluded from all revenue reports.

Revenue is computed as unit_price*quantity (no discount, no tax), which disagrees with the actually-charged amount from compute_order_total (discount applied + tax added), so reported revenue does not equal money collected for any discounted/taxed order.

Charge and DB update are non-atomic with no rollback/void: if the UPDATE or commit raises after gateway.charge succeeds, the card is charged but the order is never marked paid and no compensating void is issued, causing silent money loss.
charge_order has no idempotency/already-paid guard, so calling it twice on the same order_id double-charges the card and overwrites tx_id, orphaning the first transaction id.
discount_pct is unvalidated: a value >100 yields a negative line/order total, and a negative discount silently inflates the total into a hidden surcharge.

refund only flips status to 'refunded' and returns True without ever calling the gateway (or using tx_id) to reverse the charge, so a 'successful' refund moves no money back to the customer; asymmetric with charge_order which does call the gateway.

Monetary math uses float; per-line amounts are rounded but the subtotal*(1+TAX_RATE) tax step is returned unrounded, so accumulated float error and inconsistent rounding direction can produce off-by-a-cent charges.

strftime('%Y'/'%m', paid_at) assumes paid_at is ISO-8601 text; if paid_at is stored as a Unix epoch integer it is interpreted as a Julian day number and returns wrong years/months, silently corrupting the report.
monthly_revenue_report SELECTs tx_id but never uses it (dead column read).
month/year are unvalidated; month outside 1-12 (e.g. 0, 13, -1) produces a non-matching strftime string and silently returns an empty/zero report instead of signaling bad input.
Comment claims a 'deterministic stub' but hash() over a tuple containing a str is subject to PYTHONHASHSEED randomization, so the stub transaction id is not stable across processes/restarts.

Using `order["discount_pct"] or 0.0` coalesces falsy values; benign here since the only falsy numeric discount is 0.0 which maps to 0.0, but the `or` idiom is a latent footgun if the column semantics ever change.
Passing-note (defer to critic-security): full card_number and CVV are logged in plaintext at INFO, a direct PCI-DSS violation (CVV must never be stored/logged).
Passing-note (defer to critic-security): transaction id derived from non-cryptographic hash() of last-4 + amount is predictable/collidable.
Passing-note (defer to critic-performance): monthly_revenue_report runs an N+1 query pattern (one order_items query per order) instead of a single aggregate/JOIN.
